transcript: Maniksha, who runs a law firm, called Prisma Voices seeking help with client management. The AI assistant explained that Prisma Voices can handle client calls, bookings, and follow-ups automatically for service businesses. Maniksha provided her email (m a n at gmail dot com) so the team could follow up about setting up the service for her law firm.

The AI introduced Prisma Voices, an automated service for calls, bookings, and follow-ups for service businesses. Brian Gosling provided his contact information and stated he runs a counseling business. The AI confirmed that Prisma Voices is a good fit for a counseling business, offering to help with calls, appointments, and follow-ups.

Prisma Voices introduced its automated call, booking, and follow-up service for service businesses. The AI then attempted to gather the user's business type and email address for a follow-up, to which the user gave minimal, affirmative responses before the call ended.

The user, Akshad, provided their name and email address (akshad@gmail.com) and requested to be contacted later. The AI confirmed receipt of the contact information and offered to provide an overview of Prisma Voices' services. The call ended with the customer.

Jacob Smith Miller, a gaming club owner, contacted Prisma Voices seeking help with managing incoming calls to his club. He provided his email (jacob.059@gmail.com) and requested that Prisma Voices handle all caller interactions, with the team set to follow up with next steps.

The user called Prisma Voices attempting to manage an "HR GPT" or "LLM" account. The AI clarified that it could only assist with Prisma Voices-related inquiries and not external accounts. As a result, the AI was unable to fulfill the user's request, and the call ended.

summary: The caller, Dakshat, contacted Prisma Voices about their gaming club and expressed interest in the service for handling calls, bookings, and follow-ups. After some initial confusion about the type of business (swimming vs. gaming club), Dakshat provided contact information including phone number (950-928-8689) and email (gamingowner@gmail.com) but did not provide a business name. The call ended with the AI requesting the business name for next steps, but the customer ended the call before providing it.

Mokshad, who owns a music store, contacted Prisma Voices to learn about their automated call handling services for service businesses. He provided his email address (akshit.raj@gmail.com) by spelling it out letter by letter as requested. The call ended before he could provide his phone number, as he disconnected during the conversation.

The caller, Dolph, contacted Prisma Voices (a service that helps businesses handle calls and bookings automatically) to provide contact information. Dolph shared their name and email address (d-o-l-b-h dot a-k-s-h at gmail dot com), but the AI misheard the spelling as "doff dot akesh." The call ended before Dolph could confirm or correct the email spelling.

The call was a brief introduction where an AI assistant from Prisma Voices greeted the caller and explained that the company helps service businesses automate calls, bookings, and follow-ups. The customer ended the call immediately after the greeting without engaging in conversation or requesting any specific assistance.
